config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but individual rights should not be treated as secondary by default, because that is how abuse and authoritarianism start. In my view, the role of society is to protect people and create fair rules, not to erase personal freedom whenever it is inconvenient. A good society balances both, but it should begin by respecting the dignity and rights of each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu itu bukan pelengkap; itu dasar supaya pemerintah dan mayoritas tidak bisa semena-mena atas nama “kebaikan bersama.” Memang masyarakat perlu aturan untuk keselamatan dan ketertiban, tetapi kalau hak pribadi mudah dikorbankan, sejarah menunjukkan itu cepat berubah jadi penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Jadi, menurut saya, kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi hanya sah jika tetap dibatasi oleh hak dasar setiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual, because that is a dangerous way to think. From my perspective as someone from Indonesia, I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to silence criticism, pressure minorities, or excuse unfair treatment, so individual rights need real protection, not just promises. Society does matter, especially for public order and mutual responsibility, but its interests should be balanced against rights, not placed above them by default. If we ignore the individual, we do not build a healthy society; we build a compliant one.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu harus menjadi batas utama, bukan dikalahkan oleh kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan mayoritas menekan minoritas atau pemerintah melampaui wewenangnya, dan saya tidak mau memberi ruang besar untuk itu. Memang masyarakat butuh aturan, keamanan, dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi itu sah hanya kalau tetap menghormati hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan kebebasan beragama. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling patuh, melainkan yang paling mampu melindungi martabat tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically come before the individual, because in Indonesia that kind of thinking can too easily become a cover for pressure, conformity, and abuse. Yes, public order and shared responsibility matter, but I have seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or ignore minority rights, and I do not trust that logic when it is given priority by default. A healthy society protects people first and then asks them to contribute to the common good, not the other way around. If individual rights are weak, the society may look orderly, but it is not truly just.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, itu terlalu mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk membungkam minoritas, melemahkan kebebasan berbicara, atau membiarkan pemerintah melampaui batas. Tentu masyarakat butuh aturan dan tanggung jawab bersama, tetapi aturan itu hanya sah kalau tetap tunduk pada hak dasar seperti proses hukum, kebebasan beragama, dan perlindungan dari penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang baik bukan yang paling menuntut kepatuhan, melainkan yang paling konsisten melindungi martabat tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
